gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/enabled_by.snippet: tph-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006600 (C. elegans tryptophan hydroxylase), enabling the serotonin-biosynthetic activity in the ADF neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/enabled_by.snippet: ser-6 resolved to WB:WBGene00021897 (C. elegans octopamine receptor), enabling the octopamine receptor activity in the AWB neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/enabled_by.snippet: mod-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00003386 (C. elegans serotonin-gated chloride channel), enabling the serotonin receptor activity in the URX neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/enabled_by.snippet: tbh-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006541 (C. elegans tyramine beta-hydroxylase), enabling the octopamine-biosynthetic activity in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/enabled_by.snippet: nhr-76 resolved to WB:WBGene00015497 (C. elegans nuclear hormone receptor), enabling the transcription-factor activity in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/atgl1/enabled_by.snippet: ATGL resolved to atgl-1 / WB:WBGene00015484 (C. elegans adipose triglyceride lipase), enabling the triacylglycerol lipase activity in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/molecular_f.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (rate-limiting enzyme of serotonin biosynthesis).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/molecular_f.snippet: ser-6 is annotated (IDA) to octopamine receptor activity, a G protein-coupled octopamine receptor.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/molecular_f.snippet: mod-1 is annotated (IDA) to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity, a serotonin-activated ligand-gated anion channel.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/molecular_f.snippet: tbh-1 is annotated (IMP) to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity, the enzyme converting tyramine to octopamine.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/molecular_.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to nuclear receptor activity (ligand-dependent DNA-binding transcription factor).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/atgl1/molecular_.snippet: atgl-1 is annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity, the lipase that mobilizes stored triglyceride.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to serotonin biosynthetic process; produces the 5-HT signal in the ADF neuron.
Reactome C. elegans pathway 'Serotonin and melatonin biosynthesis' corroborates tph-1's role in serotonin biosynthetic process.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 is annotated (IDA) to octopamine signaling pathway; receives the octopamine signal in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 is annotated (IDA) to serotonin receptor signaling pathway; transduces the 5-HT signal in URX.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 is annotated (IMP) to octopamine biosynthetic process; produces the octopamine signal in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II; the TF endpoint regulating the intestinal lipolysis program (atgl-1).
gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/part_of.term_label: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure1-006/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 is annotated to triglyceride catabolic process; this is the figure's 'β-oxidation / Fat loss' endpoint downstream of ATGL.
Reactome C. elegans pathway 'Triglyceride catabolism' corroborates atgl-1's role in triglyceride catabolic process (the figure's β-oxidation/Fat loss endpoint).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/occurs_in/c.snippet: tph-1 written above the ADF blue triangle inside the NEURONS region in Panel E; ADF is a serotonergic neuron (specific cell type ADF, grounded to generic neuron CL:0000540).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/occurs_in.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to neuron projection.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/occurs_in/c.snippet: ser-6 written beside/above the AWB green triangle inside the NEURONS region in Panel E (specific cell type AWB, grounded to generic neuron CL:0000540).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/occurs_in.snippet: ser-6 (octopamine GPCR) is annotated to plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/occurs_in/c.snippet: mod-1 written above the URX yellow hexagon inside the NEURONS region in Panel E (specific cell type URX, grounded to generic neuron CL:0000540).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/occurs_in.snippet: mod-1 (serotonin-gated chloride channel) is annotated (IDA) to plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/occurs_in/c.snippet: tbh-1 written below the RIC red pentagon inside the NEURONS region in Panel E (specific cell type RIC, grounded to generic neuron CL:0000540).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to nucleus, consistent with its nuclear-receptor/TF role in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/atgl1/occurs_in.snippet: atgl-1 is annotated (IDA) to lipid droplet, the site of triglyceride mobilization in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/has_output/.snippet: tph-1's serotonin biosynthetic activity produces serotonin (5-HT), the signal labeled on the ADF→URX arrow in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/has_output/.snippet: tbh-1's octopamine biosynthetic activity produces octopamine (Oct), the signal labeled on the RIC→AWB arrow in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/has_small_m.snippet: mod-1 is a serotonin-gated chloride channel; serotonin (5-HT) is the activating ligand that gates the channel in URX.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/has_small_m.snippet: ser-6 is an octopamine receptor; octopamine (Oct) is the activating ligand received in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/has_input/.snippet: Panel E shows a solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL in the intestine; nhr-76 (nuclear receptor/TF) regulates transcription of its target gene atgl-1.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tph1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E: solid pointed arrow from ADF (tph-1) to URX (mod-1) labeled '5-HT'. tph-1's serotonin biosynthesis is causally upstream of, positive effect, on mod-1's serotonin-gated channel activity, mediated by the serotonin (5-HT) it produces (captured by tph-1 has_output serotonin / mod-1 serotonin activator). Cross-cell (ADF→URX) with diffusion intervening.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/tbh1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E: solid pointed arrow from RIC (tbh-1) to AWB (ser-6) labeled 'Oct' (octopamine). tbh-1's octopamine biosynthesis is causally upstream of, positive effect, on ser-6's octopamine receptor activity, mediated by the octopamine it produces (captured by tbh-1 has_output octopamine / ser-6 octopamine activator). Cross-cell (RIC→AWB).

gomodel:run-figure1-006/ser6/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E: solid pointed arrow connecting AWB (ser-6) to the ADF (tph-1) region; no intermediate molecule labeled. Interpreted as ser-6's octopamine-receptor signaling being causally upstream of, positive effect, on tph-1's serotonin biosynthesis. LOW CONFIDENCE (intent confidence 0.3; flagged unconfirmed against figure) — curator should verify direction and existence of this arrow.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/mod1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E: dashed line with a T-bar/bar end between URX (mod-1) and RIC (tbh-1). Interpreted as mod-1's URX activity being causally upstream of, negative effect, on tbh-1's octopamine biosynthesis in RIC. LOW CONFIDENCE (intent confidence 0.3; direction and end-type ambiguous, flagged unconfirmed) — curator should verify glyph and sign.

Panel E: dashed arrow labeled 'endocrine signal' crossing from URX/NEURONS to INTESTINE, with secreted-molecule symbols near the URX hexagon. Modeled as the compartment-level NEURONS→INTESTINE endocrine edge between the most plausible endpoints: mod-1 (URX, upstream-most neuronal node feeding the intestine) causally upstream of, positive effect, on nhr-76 (intestinal nuclear receptor/TF). Inter-tissue transport intervenes.

gomodel:run-figure1-006/nhr76/causal/gom.snippet: Panel E: solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL in the intestine. nhr-76 (nuclear receptor/TF) indirectly positively regulates atgl-1's triacylglycerol lipase activity — 'indirect' because transcription + translation of atgl-1 (already captured by nhr-76 part_of regulation of transcription + has_input atgl-1) are the intervening module.
